text.title: Jenkins Pipeline Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Jenkins pipeline patterns. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Pipeline runs for 45 minutes then fails with `java.io.NotSerializableException: java.util.regex.Pattern` at a random CPS checkpoint. Re-running succeeds maybe once in three.
antipattern.root-cause: Author stored a non-Serializable object (Pattern, BufferedReader, JsonSlurperClassic) in a local variable that crosses a CPS checkpoint (sh / sleep / input).
antipattern.fix: Wrap the offending code in `@NonCPS def helper() { ... }` returning only primitives. Never store non-Serializable references in Pipeline scope; instantiate inside `@NonCPS` blocks and consume the result immediately.

antipattern.symptom: Library author pushes a change to `main`. Within 5 minutes, 50 production pipelines start failing with new behaviour nobody asked for; rollback requires force-pushing the library repo.
antipattern.root-cause: Pipelines reference `@Library('platform@main') _`; implicit loading is enabled in `Configure System`.
antipattern.fix: Disable implicit loading. Force every pipeline to `@Library('platform@v1.4.2') _` with a real semver tag. Bump tags via PR + library smoke test.

antipattern.symptom: Unit + integration tests run in parallel; flaky `FileNotFoundException` or corrupted `target/` reports. The bug never reproduces locally.
antipattern.root-cause: Branches share the default workspace; both write to `target/` simultaneously.
antipattern.fix: Wrap each branch in `dir("branch-${env.STAGE_NAME}")` (or `ws()` for a fresh workspace). For shared external resources (DB, registry credential), wrap the affected step in `lock(resource: 'shared-db')`.

antipattern.symptom: One runaway test consumes 16 GB on a 4-node K8s cluster, evicting all other build pods. SREs spend hours diagnosing why "the cluster broke".
antipattern.root-cause: Pod template defines containers without `resources.requests` and `resources.limits`.
antipattern.fix: Set both `requests` (typical: 100m CPU / 256Mi memory for lightweight containers, 500m / 512Mi for builds, 1000m / 1Gi for docker:dind) and `limits` (2-4× requests). Pre-commit lint rejects pod templates missing either.
